comment: Wholesale (foodservice) pair register, parallel to pairs.json but separate: wholesale rows never enter the retail history. Sources: JJ Foodservice (/product/england/<SKU> pages, schema.org JSON-LD offers per branch; Collection price at the Enfield EN-MW branch is the canonical price, ex-VAT sitewide per its T&Cs 5.4) and Brakes (product pages, ng-state blob; anonymous indicative price, 'average customer discount', nettPrices=false). 'amount' is the hand-verified total pack weight/volume (from the distributor's own pack-size field, cross-checked against its published per-kg unit price on 2026-07-07); per-100 figures are computed from it. Probed and curated 2026-07-07: Booker and Costco UK are bot-blocked at the Akamai edge, Bidfood is account-gated end to end; JJ and Brakes are the two publicly fetchable distributors. Neither JJ nor Brakes lists a vegan coleslaw; JJ lists 28 beef burgers and no beef-style plant burger (its plant burgers are vegetable or coated chicken-style); Brakes lists no vegan mayonnaise. AHDB publishes deadweight (carcase) prices only, no plant-based wholesale series exists.

pairs.note: Butcher-style tier both sides. Pack weight from the page's own statement (eight sausages per pound, 64 per box = 8lb). The Cumberland variant SGS035 was rejected: its stated weight (1x3.63kg) contradicts its stated count (1x60 at 6 per pound = 4.54kg). Meat side was out of stock at all branches at curation; its price is still published.

meat.label: JJ Frozen UK Halal Minced Beef 10% Fat 2x1kg

pairs.note: Both frozen, own-brand tier. Beef side is 10% fat: JJ lists no 20% fat mince (the retail comparator tier), and leaner mince is dearer, so this ratio flatters the plant side relative to the retail convention.
Same 1kg pack both sides, both fully cooked frozen. The beef meatball declares 55% beef content.

meat.label: Paragon Halal Fully Cooked Battered Chicken Breast Fillets 18x115g

pairs.note: Coated chicken-style burger pair (buttermilk-coated mycoprotein vs battered whole-muscle chicken breast), both fully cooked frozen catering patties. JJ lists no beef-style plant burger against its 28 beef burger lines.
Same brand both sides (mirrors the retail Heinz pair) but pack sizes differ (5L vs 10L; larger packs are usually cheaper per litre, which inflates the vegan side's per-litre figure).
Same brand both sides; pack sizes differ (5L vs 10L), same caveat as the Heinz pair.

meat.label: Prime Meats British 80% Old English Pork Sausage 6s 36x76g

pairs.note: Chilled butcher-style tier both sides (branded premium plant vs 80% pork butcher-style).

Tier mismatch flagged: branded plant sausage vs own-brand traditional pork in a much larger catering pack (Brakes lists no own-brand plant sausage in a standard frozen format). Similar sausage sizes (50g vs 57g).
Both frozen foodservice lines; the beef side is the standard 20% fat tier, matching the retail comparator convention.

Comparator is pork, not beef (the only beef meatball line, Green Gourmet, no longer renders a live page and appears delisted). Both cooked. The plant side was on promotion at curation (was-price recorded by the scraper).
Comparable patty sizes (109g vs 113g), both frozen fast-food tier (own/foodservice brands both sides).
